events.text: I’m writing the compliance determination directly to `/app/answer.json` with a fail decision because unsealed duct joints at rough-complete are a code/spec defect requiring correction before closeout.

events.text: `jq` is not installed in this container, so I’m validating the JSON with the local runtime instead of changing the file again.

  "findings": [
    "Duct joints are unsealed with no approved mastic, listed foil tape, gasket, or equivalent sealing method.",
    "Cloth duct tape is not an acceptable permanent duct-joint sealing material for this commercial duct installation.",
    "Unsealed joints can cause air leakage, loss of system performance, failed pressure/leakage testing, condensation risk, and noncompliance before concealment."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop concealment of affected ductwork until joints and seams are corrected.",
    "Clean joint surfaces and seal all transverse joints, longitudinal seams, takeoffs, collars, penetrations, and connections with approved duct sealant or listed foil tape per project specifications and manufacturer instructions.",
    "Remove any cloth duct tape used as a substitute for approved sealing materials.",
    "Reinspect and perform required duct leakage or pressure testing before insulation, ceiling closure, or acceptance."
  ],
  "rationale": "The observed rough-complete ductwork has unsealed joints. Commercial duct systems are required to be substantially airtight and sealed at joints, seams, and connections using approved materials and workmanship. The condition does not meet IMC 603 intent, SMACNA duct construction/sealing practices, or Manual D performance assumptions, and it must be corrected before closeout or concealment.",
  "references": [
    "IMC Section 603 - Duct Construction and Installation",
    "IMC Section 603.9 - Joints, Seams, and Connections",
    "SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards - duct sealing and leakage control requirements",
    "ACCA Manual D - duct system design assumptions requiring sealed, low-leakage ductwork",
    "Project ductwork submittals and sealant/tape manufacturer installation instructions"
  ]
}
